_note: Onboarding beats metadata. Source of truth is the App Master Sheet Beats Context tab (beat-level fields) + Beat Elements tab (per-element lines), spreadsheet 1iNEdUm5vqmjk3YGEF1uMwfurcvgVRHykWUeBGHDBqcw. Wording is PROVISIONAL (Yair refines the exact lines later); the structure, the elementIds, the engine/variable/bubble flags, and the order are what to wire against. showsAsBubble false means the line is spoken but the component (strip/control) carries the words, no chat bubble. Only ONBOARD-01--FORM is variable (name), so it is the only beat that must be live Cartesia; everything else is a fixed recorded MP3 in Pro Clone V1 / sonic-3.5-2026-05-04.

beats.expectedResponse: Taps Apple, Google, or email sign-in

beats.opener: Hey. I'm your coach inside Guided Growth. Give me two minutes and we'll set up something that actually sticks.
I'd love to actually talk with you. If you let me use your mic, you can just speak.

beats.variableNote: greets by {name}, cannot be a baked MP3
beats.opener: Good to meet you, {name}. Two quick things so I can tailor this to you. How old are you?
beats.expectedResponse: Says or taps age and gender

beats.opener: Here's the idea. The first habit isn't a workout or a diet. It's just checking in with yourself. It takes a minute, and it changes everything else. Let's start yours right now.
Let's do your first check-in right now.
beats.expectedResponse: Taps or says sleep, mood, energy, stress
beats.clipReuse: reuses the daily morning state clips (state_sleep, state_mood, state_energy, state_stress)

beats.openerMode: Option A (no framing opener, the per-element control lines lead)

elements.line: Want a nudge when it's time?

beats.opener: Quick one. Have you tracked habits before, or is this new for you? Both are totally fine.
beats.expectedResponse: New, or I already track habits

beats.opener: What part of your life do you most want to work on right now? Pick the one that pulls you.
Within that, what's the piece you want to start with?
Pick the habits that feel doable. Not impressive, just doable. One you'll actually keep beats five you won't. Make your own if nothing here fits.

beats.openerMode: Option A (no framing opener, the per-element control lines lead)
beats.expectedResponse: Sets days and time per habit

beats.openerMode: Option B (keep the framing opener, then the control lines)
beats.opener: One more. An evening reflection, a couple of minutes to close the day. How do you want to do it, and when?

elements.line: Three questions to close the day.

beats.opener: Read me the habits you already track. Less is more to start, you can always build on it.
Now the days. Tell me how often each one runs and I'll fill them in.
Here's your plan. Your check-in, your reflection, and the habits you picked. Want to start here, or change anything first?
This is your week. Blank, starting today.
Best case, every day green. Every streak going strong. That would be amazing.
More likely, you land around here. Mostly green, a few misses, your streaks holding. That's a real win.
Some weeks land here. One streak survives, the rest take a hit. Still fine, you're building. We reassess.
The one thing we want to avoid is this. The empty days you never reported. Stay consistent, just report it. Even a miss counts, that keeps us going.
